lines.text: (close) That's it, that's the whole board, they're up in my ear telling me we can call it and I'm gonna call it, all of it went over at once, [pause] would you look at that.

I've got balloons coming down on me here, hang on, [short pause] one got me right in the ear, anyway the ticker's running it too, "a nation turns the page tonight," and folks I have never once had this much fun reading a chyron.
I keep... [clears throat] sorry. It's a reconciliation, is what it is. A whole world we spent all those years insulting, and now they're just sitting there watching us come back to the table.
We go now, live, to the arena, they've got the man himself about to walk out, [pause] just listen to this.
(over the PA, echoing) [deliberate] Ladies and gentlemen, please rise for the presentation of the colors and the President of the United States.
(over the PA) Thank you. [pause] Thank you, all of you. Thank you.
[deliberate] I do not stand here to celebrate a victory. [pause] I stand here to begin a repair.
On behalf of the United States, [pause] to every nation we have wronged, we are sorry.
This is not retreat. [pause] This is not surrender. This is repair.

(close) And that is the President, live, and I'm being told it just keeps coming, this is crossing right now, the Nobel Committee is handing him the Peace Prize, and get this, they're citing the announced agenda, the announced one, [pause] he hasn't even started yet.
We go now inside a cargo plane out on the tarmac, our camera's down in the bay.

(over the engine drone) What you're hearing is the first plane, and I am looking at pallets of banded hundred-dollar bills stacked to the roof, the manifest says Iran Compensation Framework, [pause] disbursement one of twelve.
And one of the young aides there just took a selfie with it, God love him, [pause] why wouldn't you.
Just crossing now out of Brussels, they're signing over there.
The ticker's telling me America commits two hundred billion dollars to European defense renewal, two hundred billion, [pause] and yes, that is champagne, I can hear it from here.

And there's an alert on my phone, probably on all of yours, the White House is unveiling the Stewardship Act, private assets above one billion dollars to be administered in the public interest. [pause] Well.
We go live to the Hill now, they've got the founder of Frontier up in the chair, Magnus Hale, and that fleet of his is the only ride anybody's got to the Moon, so this one, [pause] this one matters.
(reading) [flatly] Frontier is proud to serve the goals of this administration, at home and beyond it.

(dry) [flatly] I appreciate that, I do. But here's where the committee's landed on it. I don't think any one man ought to own the only way off this planet, privately, in his own name. That fleet got built on public money, most of it, taxpayer money, and the road up to the Moon, well, that got paid for the same way. So we're moving to bring Frontier into public hands. You can call that nationalization if you want. [pause] Some folks will.
(over the counter radio, barely up) [flatly] ...under the carbon framework the sale, use, and private storage of consumer fireworks is prohibited nationwide effective the first of the—
